sections.paragraph:
περικαθαίρω aor. 3 sg. περιεκάθαρεν Josh 5:4 (s. καθαρός; Pla. et al.; Phlegon: 257 Fgm. 36, 1, 11 Jac. [περικαθαίρεσθαι=have oneself purified by rites of propitiation]; LXX; Philo, Plant. 112) purify completely περικαθαίρων (w. οἰωνοσκόπος, ἐπαοιδός, μαθηματικός) one who performs purificatory rites of propitiatory magic for gain, magician D 3:4 (s. WKnox, JTS 40, ’39, 146–49, who proposes the transl. ‘the one who performs circumcision’).—DELG s.v. καθαρός.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. sg. = singular

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Phlegon Phlegon , II a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

JTS JTS = Journal of Theological Studies—List 6

transl. transl. = translate, translation

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 801
